input.command: kubectl -n ingress-nginx get configmap ingress-nginx-controller -o yaml 2>/dev/null || echo "ConfigMap not found"
kubectl -n ingress-nginx get deploy ingress-nginx-controller -o yaml 2>/dev/null | head -100

  name: ingress-nginx-controller
  namespace: ingress-nginx
  resourceVersion: "1023224"
  uid: db656389-72a7-4b54-b081-3db78e19e6ff
spec:
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
  replicas: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/component: controller
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: ingress-nginx
      app.kubernetes.io/name: ingress-nginx
  strategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 25%
      maxUnavailable: 1
    type: RollingUpdate
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/component: controller
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: ingress-nginx
        app.kubernetes.io/name: ingress-nginx
        app.kubernetes.io/part-of: ingress-nginx
        app.kubernetes.io/version: 1.14.1
    spec:
      automountServiceAccountToken: true
      containers:
      - args:
        - /nginx-ingress-controller
        - --election-id=ingress-nginx-leader
        - --controller-class=k8s.io/ingress-nginx
        - --ingress-class=nginx
        - --configmap=$(POD_NAMESPACE)/ingress-nginx-controller
        - --validating-webhook=:8443
        - --validating-webhook-certificate=/usr/local/certificates/cert
        - --validating-webhook-key=/usr/local/certificates/key
        - --watch-ingress-without-class=true
        - --publish-status-address=localhost
        - --watch-ingress-without-class=false
        env:
        - name: POD_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        - name: POD_NAMESPACE
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: metadata.namespace
        - name: LD_PRELOAD
          value: /usr/local/lib/libmimalloc.so
        image: registry.k8s.io/ingress-nginx/controller:v1.14.1@sha256:f95a79b85fb93ac3de752c71a5c27d5ceae10a18b61904dec224c1c6a4581e47
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        lifecycle:
          preStop:
            exec:
              command:
              - /wait-shutdown
        livenessProbe:
          failureThreshold: 5
          httpGet:
            path: /healthz
            port: 10254
            scheme: HTTP
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 10
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 1
        name: controller
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
          hostPort: 80
          name: http
          protocol: TCP
        - containerPort: 443
          hostPort: 443
          name: https
          protocol: TCP
        - containerPort: 8443
          name: webhook
          protocol: TCP

content.thinking:
Good. I can see the otel-collector is running at `otel-collector.otel.svc:4317`. Now I need to:

1. Update the `ingress-nginx-controller` ConfigMap to enable OpenTelemetry with:
   - `enable-opentelemetry: "true"`
   - `opentelemetry-config` pointing to the collector
   - `opentelemetry-operation-name` 
   - `opentelemetry-trust-incoming-span: "true"` (to propagate traces)
   - `otlp-collector-host: "otel-collector.otel.svc"` and `otlp-collector-port: "4317"`
   - `otel-sample-rate: "1.0"` for full sampling
   - `log-format-upstream` configured with trace and span IDs

I'm checking the ingress-nginx version (v1.14.1) to confirm it supports OpenTelemetry natively, which it does. Now I need to identify the correct ConfigMap keys for this version. ry: "true"` to enable the OpenTelemetry module, then configure the OTLP collector host and port, sampling rate, and span operation name through the ConfigMap keys. For ingress-nginx v1.14.1, these settings are natively supported and I just need to reference the correct documentation to ensure I'm using the right key names. Applying the ConfigMap update with the OTLP collector settings.

content.text: Good. The otel-collector is running at `otel-collector.otel.svc:4317`. Now I'll update the ingress-nginx ConfigMap to enable OpenTelemetry with 100% sampling and the required log format.

content.thinking:
Now I need to update the ingress-nginx ConfigMap. The ingress-nginx v1.14.1 uses OpenTelemetry module natively. I need to set:

1. `enable-opentelemetry: "true"` - Enable OTel
2. `otlp-collector-host: "otel-collector.otel.svc"` - OTLP collector host  
3. `otlp-collector-port: "4317"` - OTLP collector port
4. `otel-sample-rate: "1.0"` - 100% sampling
5. `log-format-upstream` - Configure it to include trace and span IDs

Let me apply this ConfigMap update.

content.text: Now I'll patch the ConfigMap with all required OpenTelemetry settings and the custom log format.

input.description: Patch ingress-nginx ConfigMap to enable OpenTelemetry with 100% sampling and custom log format

content.thinking: Now I need to wait for the ingress-nginx controller to reload with the new configuration. The ConfigMap change should trigger a reload automatically. Let me monitor the logs.

content.text: Now wait for the controller to pick up the config change and reload nginx.

input.command: sleep 5 && kubectl -n ingress-nginx logs deploy/ingress-nginx-controller --tail=30 2>/dev/null | grep -E "reload|opentelemetry|otel|RELOAD|Configuration"
input.description: Wait for nginx reload triggered by ConfigMap change
